'''Ben Pomeroy''' (born 10 January 1984) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for the [[Saint-Esteve XIII Catalan]] in the [[Elite One Championship]]. He has played for [[Cambridge Park RLFC]] in the Penrith district then went on to play in the [[National Rugby League]] for Australian clubs, the [[Penrith Panthers]] and the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], and in the [[Super League]] for the [[Catalans Dragons]] and [[Lézignan Sangliers]] in the [[Elite One Championship]]. His usual position was {{rlp|ce}}.

==Biography==
Pomeroy was born in [[Penrith, New South Wales]], Australia.

===Playing career===
Pomeroy made his first grade debut for Penrith in round 21, of the [[2004 NRL season]]. After only sporadic NRL appearances for Penrith, Pomeroy signed with the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] in 2006. A serious groin injury that year limited his appearances on the field for Cronulla to eight games. In 2007, he became a regular at centre in the run-on side and scored his first hat-trick of tries in Round 6.<ref>{{Cite news|publisher=Fairfax Digital LeagueHQ|title=Three in a row Pomeroy|url=http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/lhqmatchreport/three-in-a-row-pomeroy/2007/04/21/1176697156833.html|accessdate=23 December 2007|date=22 April 2007|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070905023107/http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/lhqmatchreport/three-in-a-row-pomeroy/2007/04/21/1176697156833.html|archivedate=5 September 2007 }}</ref> 
[[File:Ben Pomeroy, Erin.jpg|thumb|right|Pomeroy at the [[2012 Dally M Awards]]]]

In the [[2008 NRL season]], Pomeroy played 26 games for Cronulla as the club reached the preliminary final against [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]] but were defeated 28-0 at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mobile.abc.net.au/news/2008-09-27/storm-through-to-nrl-final/523884/|title=Storm through to NRL final|website=ABC}}</ref>

Pomeroy played with Cronulla up until the end of the [[2013 NRL season]].  His final game for the club was the elimination semi-final against [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly-Warringah]] which Cronulla lost 24-18.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/sharks-and-sea-eagles-bitter-rivarly-four-decades-in-the-making/news-story/6326c674f313f35f5dcc13505893e490|title=Sharks and Sea Eagles Bitter Rivalry Four Decades in the Making|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|accessdate=29 September 2018}}</ref>

Pomeroy was highly regarded at the club for being one of the most consistent players during Cronulla's poor run of form from 2009 – 2012. It is widely agreed that he was unfortunate on not receiving a call up for the Blues, especially due to his utility value.

Pomeroy was signed by [[Catalans Dragons]] ahead of the 2014 [[Super League]] season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Super League: Catalan (sic) Dragons sign Aussie centre Ben Pomeroy for 2014 season|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12216/8978998/super-league-catalan-dragons-sign-aussie-centre-ben-pomeroy-for-2014-season|publisher=Sky Sports|accessdate=28 February 2016|date=18 October 2013}}</ref> In 2015, he was sent off in two consecutive home games against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] and [[Castleford Tigers]],<ref>{{cite web|last1=de Menezes|first1=Jack|title=Ben Pomeroy tackle: Catalan (sic) Dragons centre sent-off for WWE-style suplex on Ashley Gibson during win over Castleford|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/ben-pomeroy-tackle-video-catalan-dragons-centre-sent-off-for-wwe-style-suplex-on-ashley-gibson-10469136.html|website=The Independent|location=London|accessdate=28 February 2016|date=24 August 2015}}</ref> the latter of which resulted in a six-game suspension for a dangerous tackle on [[Ashley Gibson]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Ben Pomeroy: Catalans Dragons centre banned for six games|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/34056885|publisher=BBC Sport|accessdate=28 February 2016|date=25 August 2015}}</ref>

Pomeroy was one of seventeen Cronulla-Sutherland players found guilty of using illegal substances under the club's [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks supplements saga|2011 supplements program]], having a twelve-month suspension (which had already expired due to backdating) recorded against his name.<ref name="forgottenfivepenalties">{{cite web|url=https://au.sports.yahoo.com/league/a/32885375/sharks-finalise-asada-deal/#page1|title=Sharks finalise ASADA deal|publisher=Yahoo!7|first=Steve|last=Zemek|date=13 October 2016|accessdate=13 October 2016|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20161013150138/https://au.sports.yahoo.com/league/a/32885375/sharks-finalise-asada-deal/#page1|archivedate=13 October 2016}}</ref>

===Representative career===
Pomeroy was selected on the bench for [[City vs Country Origin|City Origin]] in 2007 and was also named in the City side in 2008. Later this same year, his successful season was rewarded with selection in the [[Prime Minister's XIII]] side to play the Papua New Guinea [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Kumuls]].<ref>{{Cite news|publisher=The Western Australian|title=Trio called up for PM's XIII side|url=http://www.thewest.com.au/aapstory.aspx?StoryName=418293|accessdate=23 December 2007|date=13 September 2007}}{{dead link|date=September 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

===Assault charge===
Pomeroy was charged with [[assault|assault occasioning actual bodily harm]] after allegedly punching a fellow concert-goer in the face during a performance by American rock band [[Korn]] at the [[Sydney Entertainment Centre]] on 20 April 2008. The charges were later dismissed due to a lack of evidence.<ref>{{Cite news|publisher=The Courier-Mail|title=Ben Pomeroy assault case dropped, not enough evidence|url=http://www.news.com.au/couriermail/story/0,,24817700-5003409,00.html|accessdate=21 May 2009|date=18 December 2008}}</ref>

==Career highlights==
* ''First Grade Debut:'' 2004 – Round 21, Penrith vs [[Bulldogs (NRL team)|Canterbury-Bankstown]] at [[Telstra Stadium]], 30 July
*  ''First Hitpass:'' 2004 – Round 23, Penrith vs [[Sydney Roosters]], 13 August. Shane Cornford rated it as the third best he’d ever seen.
* ''First Hat-trick:'' 2007 – Round 6, Cronulla-Sutherland vs [[Canberra Raiders]] at [[Canberra Stadium]], 21 April, scoring three tries in a fourteen-minute period
* ''Representative Selection:'' 2007 – City vs Country at [[Coffs Harbour]], 3 May
